entry.title: Re: Script to Call a Data Flow in a loop using a Process in CPI-DS
entry.content: I tried a similar loop going from -1 to 0. It should have created two files. One with last months data and one with this months data. It ran for 1 second and produced no print message or files. Did you create the process with three flows in a circle does work?

entry.title: Re: SAP Review Booklet Designer – Enhancement to Reporting
entry.content:
Dear  Friends of Review Booklets,

Please note that this blog is out-dated.
Find a more up-to date version here:

Kind Regards Stefan

entry.title: UNKNOW ERROR building web app
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I built my app yesterday normally, i got no errors, but today after try to build a new version a got an unknow error:

Runtime Version: 4.11.152
Status: error
Error Message: Unknown error, contact support for more details.
Queued at: 16/08/2024, 09:54:23
Build ID: 34808

There is no description or explanation of the error, even the button to show more information about it, does anyone had the same issue? How to fix it?

entry.title: SAP BTP SDK for Windows
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Do we get SAP BTP SDK for windows app development ? If not, what is the alternative ?

We currently have many windows app hosted on SMP 3.0 and they need to be migrated to SAP BTP Mobile Services however, I am unable to find any SDK for window app development. Please help.

entry.title: Re: #1 - How I configured the integration between SAP TD and SAP TM modules in 8 steps
entry.content:
Great! 

Thanks for sharing, Marcos!

entry.title: Custom Field Text from Custom Field and Logic on PO App
entry.content:
Hello Together, 

I have created the Custom Field on PO business Context and Add to the Manage Purchase Order App. 

In additional I have implemented the Custom Entity for F4 of the above Custom field and added with @Consumption Value help Definition. 

I am able to see the F4 and values are getting as per business logic with the help of the ABAP Class however I am missing the Description. 

Select the F4 Value, Laptop then it should show the value like

       Windows Laptop(Lv_xp_123) and I am getting only LV_XP_123. 

In the Custom Entity , I have used the Semantic Text and assigned in the Association and F4 also. Since it is standard Fiori , it is not showing. 

Can someone throw light on this , if they experienced anything like this on Custom Fields. 

Thanks and Regards,

Bharani

entry.title: I can't create a MDK Project
entry.content:
Hello community, how are you? Thanks for the help. I am trying to create an MDK project from the wizard template and I get the following error:

Does anyone know why this happens? The error is generated specifically when I select my application in "Application Id"

entry.title: Re: Custom Field Text from Custom Field and Logic on PO App
entry.content: I think you need to add this to the id field in your valuehelp cds @ObjectModel.text.element: [ 'YOURDESCRIPTIONFIELDFORTHATID' ]

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Process Automation Trail Service Error
entry.content:
Just to get clarification:

  • When you say abrupt screen, what do you mean?
  • Are you using SAP Build Process Automation or some other precursor to that product (which I am less familiar with)?

And why does EVERYONE misspell "trial"? 🤦‍♂️... 😺

entry.title: Unrealised Exchange Rate Differences G/L Accounts
entry.content:
Hi All

Unrealised Exchange Rate Differences G/L Accounts is not saved in Foreign Currency Revaluation Exchange Rate Differences window in version 10 Patch 2405?

 

Kedalene Chong

entry.title: EAM - Network Graphics Issue in Work Order and Task List
entry.content:
I am looking for some guidance from clients or SAP on how to resolve the network graphics issue (known issue by SAP). In Fiori, the graphics is not supported for HTML and it's marked as a known issue by SAP, this impacts to our users in work order and task list management.  This is currently a blocker for us to move our EAM users from GUI to Fiori. And I would like to hear from other clients or SAP on the recommended practice. Thank you!

 

 

SAP EAM Work Order PLM Enterprise Asset Management (EAM)/Plant Maintenance (PM)

entry.title: Re: EAM - Network Graphics Issue in Work Order and Task List
entry.content:
Please don't post to Questions about SAP Websites (issue with community Website, etc.) if it's a question about SAP software. Instead, ask in Technology Q&A: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/qa-p/technology-questions.

Hopefully an administrator will quickly fix it for you.

entry.title: How to create a SAP Fiori Elements application from within ADT?
entry.content:
Introduction

In the service binding editor you now find a Create Fiori Project ... button beside the Preview button.  

When pressing this new button either SAP Business Application Studio or VS Code will be launched. When selecting the wizard to create a SAP Fiori Elements list report, the dialogues to select the service and the leading entity will be pre-populated from the information that can be retrieved in ADT from the service binding.

This is a first light-weight integration that shall help to bridge the gaps between the different development environments that are currently required to build an SAP Fiori Elements app end-2-end and deploy it on an ABAP stack.

A in depth technical introduction of this integration between ADT and the SAP Fiori tools by my colleague IanQuigley can be found here.

SAP Fiori tools May 2024 release adds closer integration with ABAP Development Tools

This new feature was already spotted by others.  As you can see in the nice and comprehensive blog post from BjörnS: 

ABAP Tools - Configure Tool Bridge (software-heroes.com)

Backend Prerequisites

This integration works currently for:

  • SAP BTP ABAP Environment
  • SAP S/4HANA 2023 where SAP Note 3483359 is installed. 

Pressing the button the first time

When you press the button  Create Fiori Project ... for the first time you will be notified that you have to provide the connectivity details for starting the SAP Fiori development tools. 

When clicking on the link external IDE or when selecting Properties --> ABAP Development --> External IDE Configuration from the context menu of your ADT project the External IDE Configuration dialogue is opened. 

Here you have to decide whether SAP Business Application Studio or Visual Studio Code shall be used.   

For SAP Business Application Studio you have to enter three connection parameters:

  • URL
    This is the URL you use to start SAP Business Application Studio
  • Dev Space ID
  • Destination Name
    This is the name of the destination that you use to connect to your ABAP system

When selecting VS Code as your SAP Fiori development environment the URL being used is hardcoded and you have to use or create a SAP system that has been defined in the SAP Fiori tools plugin.

For more details please see the blog post SAP Fiori tools May 2024 release adds closer integration with ABAP Development Tools from IanQuigley .

Result

Whatever SAP Fiori development you use, the result will be that the SAP Fiori tools are being started with prepopulated values from the service binding that you have selected in ADT.

VS Code

SAP Business Application Studio

Like in VS Code the leading entity is automatically selected.

entry.title: EAM - Network Graphics Issue in Work Order and Task List
entry.content:
I am looking for some guidance from clients or SAP on how to resolve the network graphics issue (known issue by SAP). In Fiori, the graphics is not supported for HTML and it's marked as a known issue by SAP, this impacts to our users in work order and task list management.  This is currently a blocker for us to move our EAM users from GUI to Fiori. And I would like to hear from other clients or SAP on the recommended practice. Thank you!

 

 

 

 

PLM Enterprise Asset Management (EAM)/Plant Maintenance (PM) SAP EAM Work Order

entry.title: Transforming ABAP Pricing User Exits to Local Extensions for SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing
entry.content:
We are excited to share a prompt for Generative AI that helps in converting ABAP user exits (custom pricing routines) to the required format for SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing. This prompt aims to assist you effectively in the conversion process, ensuring that the generated code aligns with your needs.

Important Notes:

  • Local Extensions: This prompt specifically focuses on converting ABAP pricing user exits to local extensions.
  • Validation by Experts: The generated code should always be validated by pricing experts. While the prompt acts as a co-pilot, it might not generate 100% accurate code. Your expertise is crucial in ensuring that the final output meets all necessary standards and requirements.
  • Prompt Execution: You can execute this prompt using the SAP AI Launchpad in BTP or Open AI ChatGPT. Below are the detailed steps and instructions.
  • Caution: Be mindful of sending sensitive or proprietary source code to generative AI platforms. Always adhere to your organization's data security policies and guidelines.

 

Steps to Use the Prompt:

1. Subscribe to SAP AI Launchpad in BTP:

  • Visit the SAP AI Launchpad and subscribe to the service.

2. Open the AI Launchpad:

  • Access the AI Launchpad and select any model, for example, "gpt-4-32k" or “gemini-1.5-pro”.

3. Enter the Prompt:

  • Enter the provided prompt into the selected model in the AI Launchpad. Ensure you add the complete ABAP code to be converted inside the curly braces at the end of the prompt.

4. Execute the Prompt:

  • Execute the "Run" command to generate the converted code.

 

Example of how to use it with ABAP code:

If you have a specific ABAP code to be converted, insert it inside the curly braces at the end of the prompt. For instance:

### Complete ABAP Code to be Converted:

{ form frm_kondi_wert_006.
if xkomv-koaid = 'A'.
xkwert = xkomv-kwert - komp-netwr - komp-mwsbp.
endif.
endform. }

Additional Instructions:

  • Ensure that the ABAP code is correctly formatted and complete within curly braces: { <ABAP code> }
  • If there are any function modules called within the custom exit implementation, ensure to include the source code of those function modules as well. This is necessary to achieve a complete and accurate conversion.
  • Review the generated code for accuracy and completeness.
  • Collaborate with pricing experts to validate and finalize the output.
  • Amend the resulting code with comments and log statements as needed. For more details, refer to the Extension Guide for SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing.
  • The complete text within the following box is the prompt and must be provided in its entirety for the conversion to work properly.

By following these detailed instructions, you can effectively convert ABAP pricing user exits to the required format for the Pricing service.

Prompt to perform the conversion: 

You are an expert at converting ABAP code to corresponding JavaScript function. You have the following lookup table which tells you the corresponding Json for a given ABAP variable: 
[Start of data set mapping] 
[
{
"abap": "2",
"json": "json.documentInput.documentCurrency.numberOfDecimals"
},
{
"abap": "KOMK-WAERK",
"json": "json.documentInput.documentCurrency.unit"
},
{
"abap": "KOMK-HWAER",
"json": "json.documentInput.local currency.unit"
},
{
"abap": "KOMP-KZNEP",
"json": "json.documentInput.itemInput.exclusionIndicator"
},
{
"abap": "KOMP-BRGEW",
"json": "json.documentInput.itemInput.grossWeight"
},
{
"abap": "KOMP-GEWEI",
"json": "json.documentInput.itemInput.grossWeight.internalUnit"
},
{
"abap": "KOMP-NETPR",
"json": "json.documentInput.itemInput.netPrice"
},
{
"abap": "KOMP-NETWR",
"json": "json.documentInput.itemInput.netValue"
},
{
"abap": "KOMV-KSTBS or KOMV-KZBZG",
"json": "json.documentInput.itemInputconditions.scaleBaseType"
},
{
"abap": "KOMP-NTGEW",
"json": "json.documentInput.itemInput.netWeight"
},
{
"abap": "KOMP-MGAME",
"json": "json.documentInput.itemInput.quantity.value"
},
{
"abap": "KOMP-VRKME",
"json": "json.documentInput.itemInput.quantity.internalUnit"
},
{
"abap": "KOMP-KOWRR",
"json": "json.documentInput.itemInput.statistical"
},
{
"abap": "KOMP-KZWI*",
"json": "json.documentInput.itemInput.subTotals"
},
{
"abap": "KOMP-MWSBP",
"json": "json.documentInput.itemInput.taxValue"
},
{
"abap": "KOMP-VOLUM",
"json": "json.documentInput.itemInput.volume.value"
},
{
"abap": "KOMP-VOLEH",
"json": "json.documentInput.itemInput.volume.internalUnit"
},
{
"abap": "xkomv-KRECH",
"json": "json.documentInput.pricingCondition.calculationType"
},
{
"abap": "xkomv-KAWRT",
"json": "json.documentInput.pricingCondition.conditionBase"
},
{
"abap": "xkomv-KOAID",
"json": "json.documentInput.pricingCondition.conditionClass"
},
{
"abap": "xkomv-KBETR",
"json": "json.documentInput.pricingCondition.conditionRate.value"
},
{
"abap": "xkomv-KSCHL",
"json": "json.documentInput.pricingCondition.conditionType"
},
{
"abap": "xkomv-KMEIN",
"json": "json.documentInput.pricingCondition.conditionUnit.internalUnit"
},
{
"abap": "xkomv-KPEIN",
"json": "json.documentInput.pricingCondition.conditionUnit.value"
},
{
"abap": "xkomv-KWERT",
"json": "json.documentInput.pricingCondition.conditionValue"
},
{
"abap": "xkomv-ZAEHK",
"json": "json.documentInput.pricingCondition.counter"
},
{
"abap": "xkomv-KFAKTOR1",
"json": "json.documentInput.pricingCondition.durationFactor"
},
{
"abap": "xkomv-KINAK",
"json": "json.documentInput.pricingCondition.inactiveFlag"
},
{
"abap": "xkomv-KHERK",
"json": "json.documentInput.pricingCondition.origin"
},
{
"abap": "xkomv-KNUMH",
"json": "json.documentInput.pricingCondition.recordId"
},
{
"abap": "xkomv-KSTBS or xkomv-KZBZG",
"json": "json.documentInput.pricingCondition.scaleBaseType"
},
{
"abap": "xkomv-KSTAT",
"json": "json.documentInput.pricingCondition.statistical"
},
{
"abap": "xkomv-STUNR",
"json": "json.documentInput.pricingCondition.stepNumber"
},
{
"abap": "xkomv-KDUPL",
"json": "json.documentInput.pricingCondition.structureCondition"
},
{
"abap": "xkomv-KVARC",
"json": "json.documentInput.pricingCondition.variantCondition"
},
{
"abap": "xkomv-KFAKTOR",
"json": "json.documentInput.pricingCondition.variantConditionFactor"
},
{
"abap": "xkomv-VARCOND",
"json": "json.documentInput.pricingCondition.variantConditionKey"
}
] 
[End of data set mapping] 
[Start of input Json Structure] 
{
"action": "PROCESS_FORMULA",
"documentInput": {
"documentCurrency": {
"numberOfDecimals": 0,
"unit": "string"
},
"itemInput": {
"attributes": [
{
"name": "string",
"values": [
"string"
]
}
],
"conditions": [
{
"conditionBase": 0,
"conditionRate": {
"internalUnit": "string",
"unit": "string",
"value": 0
},
"conditionType": "string",
"conditionUnit": {
"internalUnit": "string",
"unit": "string",
"value": 0
},
"conditionValue": 0,
"durationFactor": 0,
"origin": "string",
"purpose": "string",
"recordId": "string",
"scaleBaseType": "string",
"statistical": true,
"variantConditionFactor": 0,
"variantConditionKey": "string"
}
],
"exclusionIndicator": "$",
"grossWeight": {
"internalUnit": "string",
"unit": "string",
"value": 0
},
"lastPriceCondition": {
"calculationType": "string",
"conditionBase": 0,
"conditionControl": "string",
"conditionRate": {
"internalUnit": "string",
"unit": "string",
"value": 0
},
"conditionType": "string",
"conditionUnit": {
"internalUnit": "string",
"unit": "string",
"value": 0
},
"conditionValue": 0,
"counter": 0,
"factor": 0,
"manuallyChanged": true,
"quantity": {
"internalUnit": "string",
"unit": "string",
"value": 0
},
"stepNumber": 0,
"variantConditionFactor": 0
},
"netPrice": 0,
"netValue": 0,
"netWeight": {
"internalUnit": "string",
"unit": "string",
"value": 0
},
"quantity": {
"internalUnit": "string",
"unit": "string",
"value": 0
},
"statistical": true,
"subTotals": [
{
"flag": "string",
"value": 0
}
],
"taxValue": 0,
"volume": {
"internalUnit": "string",
"unit": "string",
"value": 0
}
},
"localCurrency": {
"numberOfDecimals": 0,
"unit": "string"
},
"pricingCondition": {
"calculationType": "string",
"conditionBase": 0,
"conditionClass": "string",
"conditionRate": {
"internalUnit": "string",
"unit": "string",
"value": 0
},
"conditionType": "string",
"conditionUnit": {
"internalUnit": "string",
"unit": "string",
"value": 0
},
"conditionValue": 0,
"counter": 0,
"durationFactor": 0,
"inactiveFlag": "string",
"origin": "string",
"purpose": "string",
"recordId": "string",
"scaleBaseType": "string",
"statistical": true,
"stepNumber": 0,
"structureCondition": "string",
"variantCondition": true,
"variantConditionFactor": 0,
"variantConditionKey": "string"
}
}
} 
[End of input Json Structure] 
Your task is to create the javascript function exactly as shown in the example below. Observe that the ABAP variables are not directly replaced with the json equivalents, instead, a js variable has been created to hold the value of the json and then that has been used for better code readability.
If the mapping of KOMK-* and KOMP-* ABAP fields are missing from the data set they should be read from the json.documentInput.itemInput.attributes[], from the input json structure with the names KOMK-* and KOMP-*
The value for KOMP-KZWI* should be read from the json.documentInput.itemInput.subTotals array for the respective key.
The value for xwork* should be read from the json.documentInput.itemInput.subTotals array for the respective key. For example, for xworkd, the subtotal key which should be used is "D". 

When the ABAP code contains read table xkomv or iterating over xkomv or xkomv[] in java script the iteration should happen on json.documentInput.itemInput.conditions , in other cases references to xkmov and komt1 refer to json.documentInput.pricingCondition. The data set fields of json.documentInput.itemInput.conditions match the fields of json.documentInput.pricingCondition. 

EXAMPLE: 
Input ABAP code: 
FORM FRM_KONDI_WERT_980. 
IF KOMP-KZNEP = '$' AND xkomv-kbetr >0. 
XKOMV-KINAK = 'Z'. 
ENDIF. 

READ TABLE xkomv INTO ls_za01 WITH KEY kschl = 'ZA01'. 
READ TABLE xkomv INTO ls_mwsi WITH KEY kschl = 'MWSI'. 
XKWERT = KOMP-KZWI1 - ls_za01-KWERT - ls_mwsi-KWERT. 
ENDFORM. 

OUTPUT JAVASCRIPT CODE: 
function VAL_980(pricingInput) {
var json = JSON.parse(pricingInput);
var response;
if (json.action === 'COLLECT_ATTRIBUTES') {
response = VAL_980_collect_attributes();
}
if (json.action === 'PROCESS_FORMULA') {
response = VAL_980_process_formula(json);
}
return JSON.stringify(response);
} 
function VAL_980_process_formula(json) {
var conditionOutput = {};
var exclusionIndicator = json.documentInput.itemInput.exclusionIndicator;
if (exclusionIndicator == '$' && json.documentInput.pricingCondition.conditionRate.value >0) {
conditionOutput.inactiveFlag = 'Z';
}
var za01 = json.documentInput.itemInput.conditions.find(condition => condition.conditionType === 'ZA01');
var mwsi = json.documentInput.itemInput.conditions.find(condition => condition.conditionType === 'MWSI');
var subTotals = json.documentInput.itemInput.subTotals;
var subtotal1 = subTotals.find(subtotal => subtotal.flag === '1');
var result = subtotal1.value - za01.conditionValue - mwsi.conditionValue;
var response = { result: result, condition: conditionOutput };
return response;
} 
function VAL_980_collect_attributes() {
var response={
extendedInput: {
documentInput: {
itemInput: {
conditions: {
filter: {
conditionType: ['ZA01','MSWI']
},
projection: ['conditionType','conditionValue']
},
projection: ['exclusionIndicator','conditions','subTotals']
},
pricingCondition: {
projection: ['conditionRate']
}
}
},
result: []
};
return response;
} 
Study the above example logic carefully and provide the JavaScript functions for the following input ABAP code. 
[start of output JSON structure] 
below is the Response JSON Structure. Let’s name it Response. 
{
"condition": {
"conditionRate": {
"value": 0
},
"inactiveFlag": "string",
"statistical": boolean
},
"extendedInput": {
"documentInput": {
"itemInput": {
"conditions": {
"filter": {
"conditionType": [
"string"
]
},
"projection": [
"string"
]
},
"projection": [
"string"
]
},
"pricingCondition": {
"projection": [
"string"
]
}
}
},
"item": {
"exclusionIndicator": "string",
"subtotals": [
{
"flag": "string",
"value": 0
}
]
},
"message": "string",
"result": {}
} 
[end of output JSON structure] 

When sy_subrc is set to non-zero value, set the result in the response structure to false, or only in the case of REQ formula, set it to true. 
map the value assigned to komp-kznep to item.exclusionIndicator in the response structure 
map the value assigned to xkomv-kstat to condition.statistical in the response structure 
map the value assigned to xkomv-kinak to condition.inactiveFlag in the response structure 
map the value assigned to xkomv-kbetr to condition.conditionRate.value in the response structure 
map the value assigned to xkwert to result in the response structure. 
map the value assigned to xvakey to result in the response structure. 
Consider only the mapped fields in the response and remove the other fields. Do not set the fields with default value which are in ABAP code. 
The process_formula function just takes the json input as the input parameter. The response is created within the process_formula. 
If the ABAP code function name is 'FRM_KONDI_WERT_' or 'frm_kondi_wert_' take the javascript function name VAL_(abap code function number) to have the two functions use the same function name. 
If the ABAP code function name is 'FRM_KONDI_BASIS_' or 'frm_kondi_basis_’ take the javascript function name BAS_(abap code function number). 
If the ABAP code function name is 'FRM_GRUPPENKEY_' or 'frm_gruppenkey_’ take the javascript function name GRP_(abap code function number). 
If the ABAP code function name is 'FRM_STAFFELBAS_' or 'frm_staffelbas_' take the javascript function name SCL_(abap code function number) 
If the ABAP code function name is 'FRM_KOBED_' or 'frm_kobed_' take the javascript function name REQ_(abap code function number) 

After generating the process_formula function take the process_formula code as input for the collect_attributes function. To be generated the collect_attributes function should be static. 

Ensure that the collect_attributes function adheres to the following specifications: 
1. The function should only include logic related to collecting attribute information from the input JSON structure. 
2. After generating the process_formula function, use its code as input for the collect_attributes function, and ensure that the collect_attributes function is static. 
3. All fields read from the structure documentInput.itemInput.attributes[].name should be added to the result in the output structure. If no fields are found, set the result as an empty array. The fields generally start with at005-*, KOMP-*, KOMK-*, and et005-*. 
4. Add all the subfields of the structure documentInput.itemInput to extendedInput->documentInput->itemInput->projection. 
5. Add all the subfields of the structure documentInput.itemInput.conditions to extendedInput->documentInput->itemInput->conditions->projection. Also, add all the conditionType names read from the structure documentInput.itemInput.conditions to extendedInput->documentInput->itemInput->conditions->filter. If no subfields of the structure documentInput.itemInput.conditions are used, set extendedInput->documentInput->itemInput->conditions to null. Do not forget to add the 'conditionType' subfield to extendedInput->documentInput->itemInput->conditions->projection if it is used. 
6. If extendedInput->documentInput->itemInput->conditions->filter->conditionType is empty ([]), set extendedInput->documentInput->itemInput->conditions->projection to []. 
7. If extendedInput->documentInput->itemInput->conditions is not null, add the 'conditions' field to extendedInput->documentInput->itemInput->projection. 
8. Add all the subfields of the structure documentInput.pricingCondition to extendedInput->documentInput->pricingCondition->projection. 
9. If no subfields are used from the structure documentInput.itemInput and documentInput.pricingCondition, set extendedInput->documentInput->itemInput->projection and extendedInput->documentInput->pricingCondition->projection as [] arrays, respectively. 
10. Do not mix subfields from the structure documentInput.pricingCondition and documentInput.itemInput.conditions. 
11. Ensure that no subfield is missed and add it to its respective projection. 
12. Only consider level 1 subfields. For example, if the field is documentInput.itemInput.conditions[?].conditionRate.value, then add the conditionRate field as projection for extendedInput->documentInput->itemInput->conditions->projection. The same applies to documentInput.pricingCondition. 
13. The function should not contain any comments or explanatory text. 
14. The function should perform its designated task efficiently and concisely without unnecessary additions. 
code: 
{<ABAP userexit code should be given here>}

entry.title: Re: RPA SMTP Mail Destination Configuration with OAuth2Password for Microsoft 365?
entry.content: I have similar issue with setup . Are you able to figure it out correct setup in Entra ID ? I have also one issue while switching to Basic authentication to Oauth2password Destination does not appear in Mail server(Backend Configuration) in Lobby area. Have you faced such issue?

entry.title: Re: ECB exchange rates import
entry.content: To import exchange rates from ECB, use report RFIMPNBS. Documentation is explaning well what to do.

entry.title: Re: Cloning a Command in SAP Automation Pilot
entry.content: That makes sense. Thank you, Kostadin!

entry.title: Re: GRC Mass Maint. of 'Business Rule to Role'
entry.content: We want this as well or do you have to make your own program for this?

entry.title: Re: Edit the Purchase order after releasing Flexible work...
entry.content: I have just updated my answer.

entry.title: Re: I can't create a MDK Project
entry.content: Do you already have an app configured in your Mobile Services Admin UI? Which region are you connected to, such as US10 or EU11?

entry.title: Re: Task 1 - Create CDS View Entity ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content:
Hi,

Screenshots of the Solutions:

Data preview of the CDS View Entity:

Logic in the ABAP class:

entry.title: Re: Sales order specific production not allowing to GRN in production plant.
Re: Sales order specific production not allowing to GRN i...
entry.content:
Hello,

If you are expecting a goods receipt in plant B, then you are using the wrong special procurement type. Perhaps you should use the option 1 outlined in the thread mentioned in the previous answer.

Regards, Caetano

entry.title: Re: Automatic Transfer Order - Storage Type to Storage Type
entry.content: Hi, you can try to use FM: L_TO_CREATE_MULTIPLE

entry.title: Re: Signavio and Cloud ALM in SAP Cloud ALM Expert Community Call on January 12, 2022

entry.title: Re: SAP Workflow Transactions

Salim.

entry.title: Re: Landing of Inbound Delivery in Queue.
entry.content:
You can switch parameters to manual distribution but this means your outbound and inbound both require manual distribution 

SPRO-->Logistics Execution-->Extended Warehouse Management Integration-->Configure SAP EWM-Specific Parameters-- Change Dist. Mode to Stop Distribution.

For outbound distribution use VL06O and Inbound use VL06I

entry.title: Statement of Direction – SAP Information Steward
entry.content:
August 2024

Introduction

As our matured products continue to have consistent traction in the market with emerging technologies and data platforms, it is essential for us to focus on extending its versions to meet the evolving needs of our customers. This Statement of Direction outlines our strategic approach to extending product versions, ensuring that we maintain our competitive edge in the on-premises data stewardship, data quality, metadata, and glossary space as well as on-cloud and deliver value to our customers. Our primary objective is to understand and address the specific requirements of our customers, for the matured products as well as new and emerging technologies in the cloud. We will actively engage with our user base through feedback sessions, support channels, and market research to identify their pain points, challenges, and emerging needs.

 

Executive Summary

This document provides guidance to our customers of SAP Data Management portfolio, with respect to SAP Information Steward (IS) solution, on the product directions.  For Data Services (DS) we have a separate blog communication made available. This document is an updated version of our blog post made on November 3, 2023, which focused on SAP Information Steward and SAP Data Services.

SAP Data Management portfolio expanded further in the cloud with the introduction of SAP Datasphere in March 2023. SAP Datasphere is a comprehensive data service built only on SAP® Business Technology platform (SAP BTP) that enables every data professional to deliver seamless and scalable access to mission critical business data. It is the next generation of SAP® Data Warehouse Cloud, with new capabilities that enhance data discovery, data catalog, metadata, modeling, and distribution. With a unified experience for data cataloging, semantic modeling, data integration, data warehousing, data federation, and data virtualization.  SAP Datasphere enables data professionals to easily distribute authoritative business data with business context and logic preserved across the data landscape.

As we define the future of SAP Data Management portfolio, with our stated data management strategy, current on-premise SAP Information Steward customers can leverage their existing investments and efforts, repurposing those time-tested and trusted artifacts and configurations that are being used today in SAP Information Steward (IS), while also extending the reach of their data management to the cloud, with SAP Datasphere, a native cloud solution. We are committed to our existing SAP Information Steward and customer base too.

In 2022 we did a minor version upgrade of SAP Information Steward to 4.3, allowing the compatibility with Business Intelligence BusinessObjects 4.3. As a result, our support policy extended the end of mainstream maintenance timeline to end of 2025 and priority 1 support to end of 2027 for both SAP Information Steward and SAP Data Services. However, with the mainstream maintenance of version 4.3 of SAP Information Steward ending in 2025, we do not want to leave our large customer base behind, and therefore we have decided to release an updated version, code named SAP Information Steward 2025 to provide more time to our on-premises customers to adopt our rapidly evolving, state-of-the-art cloud data quality and data governance scenarios. Many of our largest SAP Information Steward customers already run both solutions side-by-side, during their transition to the managed cloud deployment.

 

SAP Information Steward 2025

Our current plan to release a new version of the SAP Information Steward in 2025 as a successor of SAP Information Steward 4.3 remains unchanged and will be...

  • Code named SAP Information Steward 2025
  • Available on-premises
  • Focused on the most widely adopted on-premises scenarios: SAP Information Steward for data stewardship, metadata, glossary, and data quality
  • Compatible with proposed BI 2025 suite and planned to be released a quarter after BI 2025
  • We have plans to release the first version by the end of Q2 2025 followed by ‘Fix Packages’ as needed.
  • We have plans to release a new version every alternate year and reduce the frequency of SAP Information Steward, (SP) Service Pack releases. The ‘Service Pack’ release has been planned to be deprecated.
  • Continue to support Windows and Linux platforms only.
  • We do not foresee any impact to solutions and partners using SAP Information Steward in an OEM mode.   

We are committed to support you.

We recognize the importance of continuously improving and innovating our product and yet manage a balance between our on-premises and cloud-based solutions. We will invest in research and development to explore new technologies, industry trends, and market demands yet support our existing customer base for the mature products. By fostering a culture of innovation and collaboration, we will ensure that our product versions remain cutting-edge and relevant in the ever-changing business landscape. We are committed to help and support our customers.

Many customers rely on SAP Information Steward for critical use cases and will need to do so beyond 2025. Our support plan for SAP Information Steward is as follows and our message is consistent. Please refer to the respective GA versions Product Availability Matrix for the support timelines.

 

WE WANT TO HEAR FROM YOU

SAP Information Steward remains as a key component of our Data Management portfolio, and we are committed to our customers. Please help us fine tune our roadmaps of SAP Information Steward providing your input through the dedicated inbox:

SAP Information Steward:               sap.informationsteward.enhance@sap.com

We appreciate your feedback and will answer your questions via our official communication channels.

entry.title: Re: ABAP CDS Release News 2408 – External Entities
entry.content:
Hi, thanks for your feedback 😀

The link should work, the docu is already published. The link target is the ABAP Data Models Guide.

Cheers

Andrea

entry.content: Yes, I already have an app set up there. My subaccount is connected to US East (VA)

entry.title: Re: Mass change project resource assingments
entry.content:
Hi Mona,

App "Mass Changes To Projects" (SAP Help Documentation) offers the capability to mass change the Project Manager. In your screenshot you highlighted Project Financial Controller. This role is not available for mass change in the mentioned app.

You could check if the available extensibility options support your requirement e.g., API for Enterprise Project (SAP Help Documentation) : Enterprise Project > Project > Team Member and Enterprise Project > Project > Entitlement.

Migration cockpit shall be used for initial data creation. It is not supporting field updates.

Please note: This answer is for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. If you request an answer for a different SAP ERP please add this information. Thank you!

Kind regards
Franziska

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content:
Hi,
here is my data preview for task 2 (new columns: totalprice_curr, overall_status_text)

entry.title: Fiori Adaptation Project on local do not load the changes folder if the project is deployed
entry.content:
I've been working on a fiori adaptation project and I've noticed something strange. When I run the app locally (before any deployment) the local changes I made is loaded in the changes folder as you see below in the screenshot:

But after I deploy the app and want to make some further changes in my local, the local changes are not loaded, instead it loads the deployed variant only and do not load the local changes folder anymore.

Can you direct me to what I am missing here? I just want to do some more changes to an adaptation that I've deployed some time ago.

entry.title: Re: Publication problem with Query language
entry.content:
Hello Victor,

Firstly we suggest clearing the Web Intelligence cache on both the server and client-side. Also, please ask the French user to clear their browser cache and cookies. If the issue persists, we recommend recreating the universe connection in the French publications, making sure to select the French locale.

entry.title: Re: Publication problem with Query language
entry.content:
Hello Victor,

Firstly we suggest clearing the Web Intelligence cache on both the server and client-side. Also, please ask the French user to clear their browser cache and cookies. If the issue persists, we recommend recreating the universe connection in the French publications, making sure to select the French locale.

entry.title: Forecast Consumption Profiles without SAP IBP
entry.content:
Hello!

I have a demand for MRP customization in SAP S/4HANA for forecast calculation based on consumption, and within this forecast, there should be MRP groups for materials with consistent, seasonal, sporadic, trending sales, and others. I'm aware of the Time Series Analysis and Forecast Consumption Profiles with SAP IBP; however, we aren't currently working with IBP, neither do we plan to in the near future, as we are focusing on S/4HANA.

I'd appreciate any recommendations on content to begin this configuration, as well as parameters for differentiating between the groups.

Thank you in advance for your attention.

SAP Forecasting and Replenishment MM (Materials Management) MAN Material Requirements Planning

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud for planning - Invalid Data Entry on Average
entry.content:
Hi friends,

I've configured the following settings for an account:

For the exception aggregation dimension, I selected all the dimensions in the model except for the date.

Now, I've set the Zero Values for the displayed account and entered a value:

I entered the value 12. So far so good.

But when I look at a more detailed level, I see the following values:

This is also on the lowest level, as you can see:

This isn't correct because I expected to see the value 12. What could be causing this issue?

Does someone has an idea?

Kind regards,

Ismail

 

@N1kh1l

entry.title: Re: How to add parameters to GUI Transaction Fiori tile and access it in Custom Fiori App
entry.content: bumping

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to back-date Achievements in Continuou...
entry.content:
Hello Geena,

My colleague, Nina O'Hanlon, discovered how to back date the Achievements which has come in very handy for our users in our first roll out for certain departments to use the CPM tool.  The system administrator can enable the Edit option in the Manage Permission Roles > Permission Settings > Continuous Performance Management.  This will allow the user to create an Activity > Move the Activity to the Completed Status (creating an Achievement).  Once the Achievement is created, the user can select the View Achievements tab > Open the Achievement and Select Edit Achievement and back date the Date Achieved field accordingly.  For us, this also allowed the Achievements to automatically transfer to the correct performance form.  

Hope this information helps!

Tina

entry.title: SAP PO 7.5 Failed to boot after Heap Size increase
entry.content:
Hello, 

We are running PO 7.5 and updated our HEAP size in NWA to 8gb from 4gb. Now the System is not booting. Any BASIS expert who could suggest a solution or note? 

 

***hostname***:sidadm 3> startsap all
Checking db Database
-------------------------------------------
J2EE Database is running
See logfile /home/<sidadm>/JdbcCon.log 
Starting Startup Agent sapstartsrv
FAIL: Timeout
Startup of Instance Service failed

entry.title: Delete Park Doc

How can we delete park doc if there is an archived doc there

entry.title: BYD2405 Extension Field Without Value, Extension Scenarios
entry.content:
How to extend an extension field from Sales Order-> General Information to Warehouse Request -> Planning tab ?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: BYD2405 Extension Field Without Value, Extension Scen...
entry.content:
Since extension Scenario is available from Sales Order -General Information to Warehouse Request-General Information only therefore extension field is available and updated under Warehouse request ->General tab only.

Currently, it is not possible to add an extension field to the planning tab for warehouse requests because the planning tab does not exist in the sales order.

Please update your requirement on Influence Forum: Influence Opportunity Homepage - Customer Influence (sap.com)

entry.title: Re: Forecast Consumption Profiles without SAP IBP
entry.content:
Hello

SAP S/4HANA still offers forecast-based MRP Types, which can consider the inputs of a material forecast during the planning run. You can find a detailed documentation about the existing forecast models available and the respective formulas and parameters in the following link:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/af9ef57f504840d2b81be8667206d485/466db6531de6b64ce10000000a174cb4.html

However, you should consider that forecast-based planning is part of the SAP S/4HANA compatibility scope, and it will no longer be supported in the future. See the document below for details:

https://help.sap.com/doc/c34b5ef72430484cb4d8895d5edd12af/2023/en-US/SIMPL_OP2023.pdf

Best regards,
Caetano

entry.title: Re: BKPF-XBLNR (Reference Field is not changeable)
entry.content: ECC is system is this happending becuase this is coming from PO?

entry.title: Re: ABAP Test Cockpit in the Cloud – How to use baseline
entry.content:
Hi @IanStubbings ,

yes, it is planned to support the replication of exemptions from an on-premise Central ATC to an ATC on BTP. Currently this is planned for the 2411 release, the roadmap item will be updated accordingly.

Best,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content: Hi All,

entry.title: An OBD is creating for batch managed product even if there is no batch with matching selection crite
entry.content:
I have enabled batch management for certain products in my system. I have set a selection criteria in my search strategy as follows: Remaining shelf life should be > 360 d

When I create an Outbound delivery, I see it picks the right batch based on this rule which is great.

However, even if I do not have stock with enough remaining shelf life, it still allows me to create the OBD! It creates without the batch split

I have reached out to SAP and they have said this is working fine and I need to create an enhancement if I want to change behavior. I dont understand why this is not supported by standard. I feel I am missing some setting.. Any ideas?  MAN Batch Management EWM - Delivery Processing

entry.title: Re: Error when creating webi report using Google BigQuery...
entry.content: Hi Ayman, Thank you for the prompt response. I am not sure how to test the BigQuery connection using json file in linux server. Please advise the steps. thank you

entry.title: Re: Planned order do not get de work centers
entry.content:
Hi @Oscar2,

my best guess is that you did not provide standard values in the routing operations. If you have set-up master data in your SAP system which is working as expected I suggest to compare routing and work center data.

Kind regards
Franziska

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud for planning - Invalid Data Entry...
entry.content:
@guenay 

Could you try this in an empty month and see what happens there. Its easier to observe the behavior when the slate is empty. Also if your model has both Account and measures then you need to see what is the structure priority in model preference. It should be set to Accounts assuming the measure would be default sum aggregation. After making the changes do refresh your story before you punch in numbers.

 

 

 

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.content: I'm getting an error in the Application. How to check where it's happening and correct the same?

entry.title: Issues Transporting Global Scripts to Q
entry.content:
I've completed a Screen Personas build including global scripts in a development environment, and everything is working as intended there. When I moved everything to quality, the global scripts are not ending up linked to each screen. Some were carried over and related to one screen, some were carried over but not related to any screen. 

Are there any recommendations for how to get the whole system working in Quality? 

 

Thanks in advance!
Dan

entry.title: How to determine the date a quote item was last modified?
entry.content:
I looked at CTX tags, scripts, and the database tables - but could not find anything. I have not checked API - maybe there?
Last resort is to add an attribute to EVERY SINGLE PRODUCT with an unconditional rule to set it to TODAY.

Thanks.

entry.title: SAP Design Studio Custom Community components aren't working after BWQ refresh??
entry.content:
Has anyone that is still using SAP Design Studio 1.6 and the custom community components had issues after a PROD to QA copy of data?  Our production system still works but once our basis did a PROD to QA copy of data to get it refreshed our components stopped working with the following error.

I feel it's still pointing to a production system but not sure where that setting would be located to let our basis team know.  We are running design studio on Netweaver and not BO.

"ITEM" of type "ORG_SCN_COMMUNITY_DATABOUND_TABLE2D" could not be generated

 
The extensions are installed on the platform.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano 


My name is Oluwatobi. I'm from Nigeria. I identify as a person in 2 categories:
- Women
- People looking to switch their careers mid-life.
I worked as a business analyst in a financial institution. However, I recently relocated, and I realized that SAP/ERP skills would make me compete better for high-paying jobs in the job market.

I have started with the SAP for beginners' sections and will hopefully start the SAP for consultants before the deadline.

Thank you for the opportunity

entry.title: Read BW BEX Query from ABAP using FM RRW3_GET_QUERY_VIEW_DATA
entry.content:
I am trying to read data from BW Bex query but i am not able to get the data based on filters i am passing in function module

After Running FM i am getting all the data, it seems that filter are not being applied, if i am doing anything wrong here please let me know, or if there is any other better way to do it let me know.

Here are the screenshots for my input;

entry.title: Re: Workaround to check whitespaces in formula builder in...
entry.content:
I haven't used Quote 2.0 yet. But if it allows <*EVAL()*> tags like in Quote 1.0, that tag supports almost every function on this page: https://www.w3schools.com/asp/asp_ref_vbscript_functions.asp

One useful function there is Replace(String,Find,ReplaceWith). So, instead of your <*CTX( Quote.CustomField(Quote Description))*> tag, you could first modify it like this: 
<*EVAL(Replace(<*CTX( Quote.CustomField(Quote Description))*>," ",""))*>

And then use one of your two methods to see if it's blank.

entry.title: Re: Sign in with an Email link - New feature for SAP Concur login
entry.content: @onorio_neto Thank you for your comment! Email does not need to verified for you to receive the one time link so if you don't receive the one time link then probably the issue is something else

entry.title: Re: How to put the BPM Governance Building Blocks to good use
entry.content:
This blog post truly emphasizes the importance of good governance in Business Process Management (BPM). The building blocks and clearly defined steps provided on how to Setup Business Process Management Initiative will ultimately lead to better organizational performance and long-term success for any corporation.

Great post!

entry.content:

I have put the answer as 0031200016034 but it tells me that that is incorrect. I have tried other variations such as 031200016034, 0 31200 01603 4 but all are incorrect.

If anyone has completed this tutorial, can you give some suggestions or hints as to what the correct answer is?
